response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said has a broad and impressive software background spanning over 15 years, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep C++ expertise as its core competency, yet the candidate's C++ exposure is limited to a single short stint at National Instruments (6 months) and a university thesis project. His career trajectory is firmly in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, DLang), with no meaningful experience in industrial automation, IoT protocols, secure development lifecycles, IEC 62443, or any of the domain-specific communication protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) the role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-performance, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and concurrent patterns
Solid event-driven and message-broker architecture experience through GISCollective and microservices work, which partially overlaps with the role's architectural requirements
Strong full-stack breadth (TypeScript, React, Node.js, AWS CDK) that satisfies several of the 'advantageous skills' listed in the JD
concerns: C++ proficiency is negligible — only 6 months at National Instruments and a thesis project, far below the depth required to architect and lead high-performance C++ platform solutions
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems domain knowledge; no familiarity with any of the listed industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No exposure to secure development lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443-4-1, or any industrial cybersecurity standards, which are listed as core security competencies for this role
